records.ety: [OE. sojornen, sojournen, OF. sojorner, sejorner, F. sjourner, fr. L. sub under, about + diurnus belonging to the day. See Journal, Diurnal.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sojourned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sojourning.]

defs: To dwell for a time; to dwell or live in a place as a temporary resident or as a stranger, not considering the place as a permanent habitation; to delay; to tarry.
quotes: Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there.
Home he goeth, he might not longer sojourn.
The soldiers first assembled at Newcastle, and there sojourned three days.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. sujurn, sujur, sejor, F. sjour. See Sojourn, v. i.]

defs: A temporary residence, as that of a traveler in a foreign land.
quotes: Though long detained In that obscure sojourn.
